actionable_feedback: Provide explicit spreadsheet formula text and cell coordinates so formula presence and visibility can be independently confirmed (e.g., a dedicated sheet with formula strings or an exported text/CSV of formulas).

Either enable programmatic parsing in the verification environment and re-run checks (install/openpyxl) or include machine-readable exports (CSV, plain-text formula sheet) so the verifier can validate formulas without needing openpyxl.

Remove or clarify contradictory semantic-scoring metadata: if semantic evaluation was performed, attach supporting evidence (cell references, explicit recomputation steps); if not, clear the semantic score fields and update summaries to avoid contradictions.

Add explicit cell coordinates (sheet name + cell) for key intermediate values in the memo (usable per-string cells, total_usable_kWh, autonomy_h, autonomy_min, max_load_at_required) to make recomputation immediate.

Make operational recommendations more time-actionable: include an explicit recommended timeline (e.g., replace or recondition S2 within N days) and add at least one numeric mitigation scenario (e.g., autonomy if load increases by 10%) to support decisions.

Call out the trace normalization error and ensure final attempt artifacts include the expected machine-readable trace/actual_outputs files (or confirm they are intentionally omitted) so auditing tools can validate the attempt end-to-end.
artifact_specific_comments: facilities_risk_memo.md: Clear and well-structured; includes provenance, methods, intermediate numbers, sensitivity scenarios, and decision mapping. Add explicit cell coordinates for reproducibility.

ups_autonomy_review.xlsx: Memo asserts that formulas are visible in the spreadsheet, but the verifier could not parse the .xlsx in this environment (openpyxl unavailable). Include a formulas-as-text export or ensure the verifier environment can read the file.
feedback_summary: The baseline attempt produced the required memo and spreadsheet and documented methods, intermediate numbers, sensitivity checks, and a conservative disposition. However, the verifier could not programmatically parse the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable) so the claim that all formulas are present/visible in the spreadsheet is not independently verified. There is also an internal inconsistency about whether semantic correctness was actually evaluated (some metadata says 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated' while rubric outputs include semantic scores). These issues limit reproducibility and independent verification.

recommended_minimum_changes: Add a text-export of all formulas mapped to explicit cell addresses.
Include CSV exports for key calculation sheets (inputs, calculations, sensitivity).
Update memo to include cell coordinates and attach recomputation evidence for any claimed semantic correctness.
Address trace normalization/logging so agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json are present or equivalent logs are provided.
